title: 17 Creating a Bank to Bank Wire
body_text:
17	Creating a Bank to Bank Wire
17.1	Overview of Creating a Bank to Bank Wire
A Bank to Bank Wire is an outgoing wire that credits another financial institution rather than a an individual customer.
To create a Bank to Bank wire.
Log in to Wires as described in Chapter 10, Logging in to Wires.
Select
Create Bank to Bank
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select Create Bank to Bank
17.2	Completing the Sending Financial Institution Section
Complete the Sending Financial Institution section as follows:
Select the sending branch from the Branch drop-down menu. If you have access to one branch, the sending branch will default. See Figure 2. Central 1 debits the branch that you select.
Note: 	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Select a Branch
17.3	Completing the Beneficiary Financial Institution Section
Complete the Beneficiary芒聙聶s Financial Institution section as follows:
Enter the routing instructions (i.e., SWIFT code, transit number, or ABA number) for the receiving financial institution in the 芒聙聹Swift/Transit #/etc.芒聙聺 field and select
Search
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Search for the Beneficiary Financial Institution
Alternatively, select
Advanced Search
to search for the receiving financial institution by name and address. See Figure 3. Enter the applicable criteria and select
Search
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Advanced Search for Beneficiary Financial Institution
Select the appropriate financial institution from the search results. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select the Beneficiary Financial Institution
A warning message displays if the destination financial institution芒聙聶s country is listed as a prohibited, tax haven / high risk, or a sanctioned country. See Figure 6. Warning messages are provided on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed.
Figure 6: Country-Specific Warning Message
Note:	If the destination financial institution芒聙聶s country is listed as a prohibited country, an error message displays after selecting 芒聙聹Next芒聙聺, and the wire won芒聙聶t be permitted to proceed. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Country-Specific Prohibition
17.4	Completing the Wire Amount Section
Complete the Wires Amount section as follows. See Figure 8.
Enter the value of the wire in the Amount field.
Select the wire currency. The currencies available in the Currency drop-down menu vary based on the beneficiary financial institution.
Figure 8: Completing the Wire Amount Section
US Dollar Wires
To create a US dollar wire:
Complete the Amount and Currency fields as previously described. The currency defaults to US dollars if the beneficiary financial institution芒聙聶s country is the United States. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Bank to Bank Wire Amount Section
Note:	When sending US dollar wires to another Wires participating financial institution, a US correspondent bank isn芒聙聶t required, the wire is routed to the Pending Incoming wires for the receiving financial institution.
Foreign Currency Wires
Foreign exchange rates apply if the selected value in the Currency drop-down menu isn芒聙聶t Canadian or US dollars.
To create a foreign currency wire:
Complete the Amount and Currency fields as previously described.
An estimated exchange rate displays. The final exchange rate is determined when completing the transaction. See Section 17.10, Confirming the Foreign Exchange Quote.
Note:	The exchange rate includes the Central 1 rate and, if applicable, your financial institutions芒聙聶 Foreign Exchange (FX) spread.
Optionally, enter the Canadian dollar value of the wire in the CAD Equivalent Amount field to calculate the estimated foreign currency value. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Wire in EUR Currency
17.5	Completing Intermediary Information
If you are sending a wire that isn芒聙聶t in the domestic currency of the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s country (e.g., sending GBP to France), the Intermediary Financial Institution field displays. Wires may also require an intermediary based on the beneficiary financial institution.
Select an Intermediary from the Intermediary Financial Institution drop-down menu. Central 1 recommends selecting an intermediary from the top of the list. See Figure 11. The intermediary defaults if a single intermediary is available.
Figure 11: Select the Intermediary Financial Institution
If Wires doesn芒聙聶t provide an intermediary for the beneficiary financial institution, contact the receiving financial institution directly to obtain the appropriate intermediary.
You must search for and select the specified intermediary when creating the wire on the Wires application. To search for an intermediary:
Select
Search by Intermediary FI Name/Address
. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Selecting 芒聙聹Search by Intermediary FI Name/Address芒聙聺
Enter the search criteria and select
Search
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Entering Search Criteria
Select the appropriate financial institution from the search results. The intermediary financial institution displays in Wires. See Figure 14. Alternatively, select
Clear
to remove the selected intermediary financial institution.
Figure 14: Selected Intermediary Financial Institution.
17.6	Completing the Wire Priority Section
The Wire Priority section displays if the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution is in Canada and the wire currency is Canadian dollars. A high-priority wire only applies to wires destined for other high-value system (Lynx) participant financial institutions (e.g., Royal Bank of Canada, Bank of Montreal), not to credit unions or other Central 1 clients. If the high-priority option is selected, wires are processed with higher priority in Lynx. See Section 1.5, Contacts, Websites, and other Resources, for a link to the other Payments Canada high-value system (Lynx) participants. Wires must be approved by an authorized user prior to being processed as a high-priority wire. Additional processing fees apply to high priority wires.
Complete the Wire Priority section as follows. See Figure 15.
Select
Regular
to send a regular priority Canadian dollar domestic wire (Regular is the default).
Select
High
to send a high-priority Canadian dollar domestic wire.
Figure 15: Completing the Wire Priority Section
17.7	Completing the Future Dated Wire Section
The Future Dated Wire section displays if the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution is in Canada and the wire currency is Canadian dollars. The Future Date field allows users to schedule the release of a wire on a specified date. Authorized users must approve future-dated wires prior to the scheduled date, or Central 1 converts the wire to a regular bank to bank wire. Additional processing fees apply to future-dated wires.
Important:
Once you approve a futured-dated wire, you can only cancel it by submitting a trace request to Central 1. Central 1 must receive requests to cancel a future-dated wire no later than 2:15 pm PT / 11:15 am ET the business day before the specified value date (future date). A cancellation fee applies. Cancellation requests are processed on a best- efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed If the wire was sent to the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution on the value date, you must initiate a recall request. See Chapter 25, Recalling, Tracing, and Amending Wires.
Important
:	Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
Complete the Future Dated Wire section as follows. See Figure 16.
Leave blank if you芒聙聶re not creating a futured-dated wire.
Select a date using the Future Date calendar widget to create a future-dated wire. You can select a date up to 14 days from the current date.
Figure 16: Completing the Wire Priority Section
17.8	Completing the Payment Details Section
Use the Payment Details section to record information about the wire for the beneficiary financial institution. For example, if the bank to bank wire is associated with another wire and it芒聙聶s being returned, enter the reference number in this field. The data that you enter in the Payment Details section travels with the payment. This can improve the accuracy and completeness of payment instructions and reconciliation, automate post-payment activities, and enable better cash flow management. See the
Prohibited Remittance Content Procedure
for content that can芒聙聶t be included in payment messages.
Complete the Payment Details section as follows:
Enter your internal reference number in the Related Reference Number field. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Enter the Related Reference Number
Related Information
Prohibited Remittance Content Procedure
17.9	Completing the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information Section
Use the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information section to record additional information about the wire for the beneficiary financial institution. Line 1 is mandatory. Complete the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information section as follows:
Enter information for the receiving financial institution in Lines 1 and 2 (e.g., the type of payment) as appropriate. Select
Next
when all applicable mandatory fields are complete. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Sender FI to Receiver FI Information
17.10	Confirming the Foreign Exchange Quote
You will be prompted to confirm a foreign exchange quote, if the wire requires a foreign exchange rate. Foreign exchange doesn芒聙聶t apply for wires sent in Canadian or US Funds.
Confirm a foreign exchange quote as follows:
Select
Confirm
when prompted to confirm the current foreign exchange quote or
Refresh
to get an updated quote. See Figure 19.
Note:	A countdown timer indicates when the foreign exchange quote will expire. If the quote expires, select
Refresh
to get a new quote.
Figure 19: Foreign Exchange Confirmation
17.11	Confirming the Wire Transaction
Review the confirmation page to verify that the wire data is correct before confirming the wire.
To confirm the wire:
Select
Create Bank to Bank Wire Transfer
to confirm the wire. See Figure 20. Alternatively, select
Edit
to make changes or select
Cancel
to cancel the creation of the wire.
Note:	Select
Print Confirmation
to print a wire confirmation per financial institution policy. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Confirm the Wire Transaction
Select
Print Receipt
to print a wire receipt if required. See Figure 21.
Note:	The wire reference number and number of approvals required displays when you complete the wire. All wires must be approved. See Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires.
Figure 21: Print Receipt
END
